item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks man, that means a lot!

I actually did try really hard to make that cave painting too, but it just kept coming out really bad. Maybe I'll try again some time when I have a more concrete idea on how to draw the big lizard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Way to take a thing what kills you and turn into a magical/genetically engineered/sasquatch/fey/war-demon…thing. I dig it.

Would’ve been cool to see that cave painting of 682 though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, thanks man, that means a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so underrated…

+1
So thrilled to hit this milestone so quickly, I'm so glad you all like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is a rewrite of the original SCP-517-ARC. I have a link for it on the first comment in this thread. The idea being since it does have regenerative abilities and strong durability that the mask wouldn't actually kill this thing, thus providing it with a permanant host, which obviously we woudn't want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It may seem strange that a series I scp would reference a series V scp, but I think its good for being something new I guess.

I believe there was a rewrite of 517, hence the oddness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The SCP-517 link from SCP-035 redirects to here. Some edits have to be made (Not on SCP-4715). Also, the original SCP-4715 link can be safely removed as long as this scp link is replaced. It may seem strange that a series I scp would reference a series V scp, but I think its good for being something new I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I'm really glad you liked it! I'm not sure where the other three attempts are, it's actually just something I've heard around chat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
glad to see the old SCP-517 get a new lease on life! judging by the fact that this is sitting at +70, it seems to have succeeded!

so this is the 4th rewrite attempt? are there archive links to the other 3, or were those attempts done within the original article before archiving?

also, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a problem. Perhaps there needs to be a prehistoric tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much! I had the idea to tie it to the children of the night for a long time, but turned it over in my head quite a bit before I felt confident in putting it to paper. I'm really glad you enjoyed it.
As for the tag, I didn't actually do those, but I guess putting dinosaurian is just easier for navigational purposes than something like "Extinct mammals" or something similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've known about SCP-517-ARC for a long time, but seeing it brought back like this makes me quite happy. I love how much history and lore you added to it, as well as the ties to the Children of the Night. Very good way to modernize an -ARC. +1

EDIT: You might need to remove the "dinosaurian" tag. I don't really see anything dinosaur related in this document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your feedback, I'm sorry it wasn't your thing, but I'm glad you liked the history and stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1. i don't have any attachment to 517-ARC, so this article for me is just another Big Unkillable Monster. the historic & religious undertones are cool, but there really isn't a hook here for me. i echo the sentiment of others here who say it's just too close to 682.

item.title: SCP Cafe!!!
item.content:encoded:
Wow! I was lucky enough to have this featured on pxdnbluesouls SCP Cafe podcast! For those who don't know, the SCP Cafe is a podcast run by bluesoul, who takes the time to read and review many SCPs every single week and feature them in the weekly podcast, where he does a full reading and gives his thoughts on them. I am really excited and humbled to have this featured there, thank you!

The link to this specific episode can be found HERE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 517-ARC was kept around due to it being mentioned in other SCPs on the site, being part of the “Referential Integrity” section. It’s like how some of the other skips under that section were saved due to being part of Incident 239-B.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ARCs are deleted once the rewrite is high enough. See 087-ARC.

Well… also when staff remember to do so. d: Feel free to poke one of us if an -ARC is due to be cleared after a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry it didn't grab you, I knew it was a bit of a risky rewrite with its inherant similarities to 682 and all, but I did try my best to make it stand on its own. I actually did try really hard to slim it down during the crit phase, but that is one area I struggle with. If you'd be willing to suggest where it could be slimmed down I'd be happy to hear :)
Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to like it, but I can't. This is a really fluffy article and could honestly be slimmed down a lot given the chance. Granted it is written well but this really did not hold my attention and I found myself getting distracted throughout to other things. Also the SCP seems too similar to 682 for my tastes.

Idk, maybe I'll come back and give it another chance but -1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with this. I did enjoy the article and upvoted it, but some of the paragraphs are quite bulky, which makes reading them a bit arduous at points. They could use some trimming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Space out your paragraphs or just shorten the content for each paragraph altogether. That's mostly what's putting it off for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ARCs are deleted once the rewrite is high enough. See 087-ARC.
I'll get to reading this either today or the day after tomorrow, I hope it stays until then!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I really hope that the original stays up. I tried hard to make sure that this can stand on its own two feet. Thank you very much for the upvote, and I'm glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let’s see. A rewrite of/skip inspired by one of the more popular ARC’ed skips that is also connected to the Children of the Night and adds more to their conflict with the Faes? The skip is also different enough to warrant keeping the original (which is also one of the “protected” ARC’s). +1.

Seriously, we need more Night Children-Fae articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! While I can't take credit for the original concept, I'm glad you liked the 1000 angle!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, I'm sorry about that. I was concerned with becoming overly wordy with it and maybe went a bit overboard condensing it down. I'm glad you liked the idea though. Would you possibly have hany suggestions on how to improve?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this idea. The concept of an ancient precursor bioweapon that was unleashed by an angry 1000 instance is absolutely excellent and executed very well. I would love to see more SCPs that examine the remnants of the Children of Night empire. It's a mythology that definitely needs expansion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not bad. It's just that the paragraphs are too condensed that it becomes distracting to read seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks dudemanbro! the hard part about the pictures was finding a cave painting of this fictional creature. Thankfully some neanderthals had the foresight to make some shit up and I could use that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this rendition of this SCP. 517-ARC was always my favourite -ARC and the name alone was striking enough for me to push for its preservation.

I love the way you executed this, the new origin story and the pictures really do this justice. A++

item.title: Shout outs, Thank yous, Credits and Image Attribution
item.content:encoded:
This is a re-write of SCP-517-ARC I actually started work on months ago, and it wouldn't have been possible without the huge amount of help I had from Ihp, Dexanote and UraniumEmpire, who provided so much crit and advice that honestly they should be credited with co-authoring the skip. I would also like to thank Roget, LurkD, ProcyonLotor, Tuomey Tombstone, ChaoSera, The Raven and Mister Madness For helping me brainstorm and offering even more crit. More thanks go out to thedeadlymoose for giving me advice on writing about SCP-1000, and giving me his blessing to reference and crosslink to it as well. Thank you to djkaktus for writing SCP-2932 which played a big part inspiring a big chunk of this rewrite, as well as AdminBright for letting me link and reference SCPs, 001 this article, a big thank you to Modern_Erasmus for helping me go about the rewrite process, and finally, thank you to HK-016 For writing the original. I hope you like it.

I always liked 517-ARC, at first just because it was a big ol angry mammalian 682, then for its cool name, but overall because I feel like it always had great potential to be truly great. For a lot of people, it was the first scp they ever saw, surviving the Mass Edit, the move to this site, and even sinking into deletion ratings. I hope that I'm able to deliver on the core themes of the article, while also bringing something new, modern and interesting. I tried to differentiate it from a lot of the other monstrous SCPs by giving it an expanded history, as well as a few new bells and whistles. To reflect that this is the 4th rewrite of 517, I have chosen the number 4715, or in other words: 517-4.

The main photograph was taken by myself, and I also made the cave painting myself. As such, I give myself permission to use these images in this Scip.

The third image was sourced from the website Unsplash, whose copyright and licensing policy can be found Here. It was taken by the very talented James Kemp, whose gallery is FULL of fantastic photographs.
